title: Tailwind CSS
content:
## 💨 Velocity in Documentation Tailwind CSS is a **utility-first** framework. Instead of writing custom CSS, you apply pre-defined classes directly to your HTML. --- ## 1️⃣ Why Tailwind? * **No context switching**: You don't have to jump between HTML and CSS files. * **Consistent design**: Tailwind uses a strict spacing and color system. * **Tiny CSS file**: Tailwind automatically removes unused classes when you build for production. --- ## 2️⃣ The Utility Syntax ```html
Click Me
``` * `bg-blue-500`: Background color. * `p-4`: Padding. * `hover:bg-blue-600`: State variants. * `rounded-xl`: Border radius. --- ## 3️⃣ Responsive Modifiers Tailwind makes media queries easy with prefixes: `w-full md:w-1/2 lg:w-1/3` (Full width on mobile, half on tablet, third on desktop). --- ## 💡 Pro Tip Use **Tailwind CSS IntelliSense** extension in VS Code to see class documentation as you type.

questions.question_text: In Tailwind CSS, what does the 'md:' prefix represent?
questions.explanation: 'md:' is a breakpoint prefix that typically targets screens at 768px and larger.
Tailwind provides low-level utility classes that you use directly in your HTML to build custom designs.

questions.question_text: How do you add a red background with Tailwind?
questions.explanation: 'bg' is the utility for background, and 'red' is the color name followed by a shade (500).

questions.question_text: What does the code 'p-4' do in Tailwind?
options: Adds 1rem (16px) of padding on all sides
questions.explanation: In Tailwind, 'p' is padding, and the number is multiplied by 0.25rem by default.

questions.question_text: How do you apply a style ONLY on hover in Tailwind?
questions.explanation: Tailwind uses 'State Modifiers' (like hover:, focus:, active:) as prefixes.

questions.question_text: How do you apply a style ONLY on medium screens (e.g. tablets) and up?
questions.explanation: Tailwind uses a mobile-first responsive system with prefixes like sm:, md:, lg:, and xl:.

options: Writing as little CSS as possible by reusing small helper classes
Creating a new CSS file for every component
questions.explanation: Instead of naming classes like '.card', you use '.rounded .shadow .p-4'.

questions.question_text: How do you center text in Tailwind?
questions.explanation: text-center is the utility for text-align: center.

questions.question_text: Which class would you use to create a Flex container in Tailwind?
How do you add a custom value (like 13px) that isn't in Tailwind's default theme?
questions.explanation: Square brackets [] allow you to use 'Arbitrary Values' in Tailwind.

questions.question_text: Why is Tailwind's file size small in production despite having thousands of classes?
options: It is delivered via a very fast CDN
It uses 'Purge' (or JIT) to remove all unused classes from the final CSS file
It doesn't actually contain any CSS
questions.explanation: Tailwind scans your HTML files and only keeps the CSS for the classes you actually wrote.

questions.question_text: How do you make an element take up half the width of its parent?
questions.explanation: Tailwind's fraction system (w-1/2, w-1/3, etc.) is highly intuitive for layouts.

questions.question_text: Which utility class adds rounded corners to an element?
questions.explanation: rounded-sm, rounded, rounded-lg, or rounded-full are common variants.

questions.question_text: How do you change the text color to white in Tailwind?
What is the purpose of the 'tailwind.config.js' file?
options: To customize the framework (add custom colors, fonts, spacing, etc.)
To run the JavaScript for the website
questions.explanation: This file is where you 'extend' Tailwind to match your specific brand design.
